methods.name: beginResize
methods.rawdescription:
Called on mousedown — hides the overlay immediately (synchronous IPC fire)
and sets the resizing signal so Angular effects can also react.
methods.description: Called on mousedown — hides the overlay immediately (synchronous IPC fire) and sets the resizing signal so Angular effects can also react.

methods.name: endResize
methods.rawdescription:
Called on mouseup — restores the overlay to the new position and clears
the resizing signal.
methods.description: Called on mouseup — restores the overlay to the new position and clears the resizing signal.

injectables.description:
Coordinates sidebar drag-resize with any native overlay (e.g. the DevTools BrowserView) that needs to be hidden/restored around the drag.

Components that manage native overlays call registerOverlayCallbacks() to supply hide/restore functions. The right-sidebar calls beginResize() and endResize() which invoke those callbacks SYNCHRONOUSLY — before any mousemove event can reach the native overlay and steal pointer capture.
injectables.rawdescription:
Coordinates sidebar drag-resize with any native overlay (e.g. the DevTools
BrowserView) that needs to be hidden/restored around the drag.

Components that manage native overlays call `registerOverlayCallbacks()` to
supply hide/restore functions.  The right-sidebar calls `beginResize()` and
`endResize()` which invoke those callbacks SYNCHRONOUSLY — before any
mousemove event can reach the native overlay and steal pointer capture.

components.name: PageComponent
components.template:
Pages in Storybook

We recommend building UIs with a component-driven process starting with atomic components and ending with pages.

Render pages with mock data. This makes it easy to build and review page states without needing to navigate to them in your app. Here are some handy patterns for managing page data in Storybook:

  • Use a higher-level connected component. Storybook helps you compose such data from the "args" of child component stories
  • Assemble data in the page component from your services. You can mock these services out using Storybook.

Get a guided tutorial on component-driven development at Storybook tutorials . Read more in the docs .

Tip Adjust the width of the canvas with the Viewports addon in the toolbar
components.sourceCode:
import { Component } from '@angular/core'; import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common'; import { HeaderComponent } from './header.component'; import type { User } from './user'; @Component({ selector: 'storybook-page', standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule, HeaderComponent], template: `

Pages in Storybook

We recommend building UIs with a component-driven process starting with atomic components and ending with pages.

Render pages with mock data. This makes it easy to build and review page states without needing to navigate to them in your app. Here are some handy patterns for managing page data in Storybook:

  • Use a higher-level connected component. Storybook helps you compose such data from the "args" of child component stories
  • Assemble data in the page component from your services. You can mock these services out using Storybook.

Get a guided tutorial on component-driven development at Storybook tutorials . Read more in the docs .

Tip Adjust the width of the canvas with the Viewports addon in the toolbar
`, styleUrls: ['./page.css'], }) export class PageComponent { user: User | null = null; doLogout() { this.user = null; } doLogin() { this.user = { name: 'Jane Doe' }; } doCreateAccount() { this.user = { name: 'Jane Doe' }; } }

@for (item of nav; track item.id) { }
@if (persisted.snapshot(); as snap) { @switch (section()) { @case ('general') {

General

Core browser behaviour and default preferences.

Behaviour

Background tabs unload to save memory; selecting the tab reloads the page.

Form autofill hints

Saved values appear in the top bar "Fill" menu to insert into email / username fields.

@for (h of snap.settings.autofillHints; track h.id) {
} @empty {

No hints yet.

}
} @case ('features') {

Features

Turn optional tools and panels on or off. Tabs, omnibox, and workspaces stay available.

@let ff = effectiveFeatureFlags(snap);
Quick launcher & modes
Toolbar
Right panel & widgets

When off, the panel rail and all widgets below are hidden.

Widgets
Data & automation

When off, new visits are not appended to history (existing entries remain).

} @case ('privacy') {

Privacy

Tracking protection, HTTPS, and clipboard controls.

Tracking & ad blocking
@if (blockListStatus()) { {{ blockListStatus() }} }

@if (bridge.isElectron) { }
} @case ('appearance') {

Appearance

@for ( t of [ { value: 'dark', label: 'Dark' }, { value: 'light', label: 'Light' }, { value: 'midnight', label: 'Midnight' }, { value: 'solarized', label: 'Solarized' }, { value: 'high-contrast', label: 'High contrast' }, ]; track t.value ) { }

Community themes

No entries (or registry not loaded).

}
}
} @case ('automation') {

Automation

Rules fire on URL navigation, workspace switch, or a local-time window.

Rule Marketplace

Curated rules maintained by the Dev-Lens team. Click Install to add to your list.

@for (mr of marketplaceRules; track $index) {

}

} } @case ('ai') {

AI Assistant

Provider
@for ( p of [ { v: 'mock', l: 'Local mock', icon: 'fa-flask', desc: 'Canned replies — no API key required', }, { v: 'openai', l: 'OpenAI-compatible', icon: 'fa-brain', desc: 'Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint', }, ]; track p.v ) { }
@if (snap.settings.aiProvider === 'openai') {

}

}

{{ row.label }}
}

Plugins

Each plugin runs in a sandboxed sidebar webview with permission-gated APIs.

@if (bridge.isElectron) {
Installation

Drop an unpacked plugin folder at userData/plugins/<id>/manifest.json (folder name must match the manifest id).

@for (p of pluginRuntime.allPlugins(); track p.id) {
{{ p.name }} {{ p.id }} · v{{ p.version }}{{ p.bundled ? ' · bundled' : '' }}

Extensions

@if (bridge.isElectron) {
How to install

@if (extLoading()) {

@if (ext.iconDataUrl) { } @else { }
{{ ext.name }} {{ ext.id }} · v{{ ext.version }} @if (ext.description) { {{ ext.description }} }
} @empty {

} } } @else {

Extensions are available in the Electron app only.

} } @case ('sync') {

Sync & backup

Dev-Lens is offline-first. Export backups to move data between machines.

Companion snapshot

Browser-agnostic JSON (bookmarks, read-later, session names) for future mobile / sync tools.

Encrypted backup

AES-GCM encrypted with a PBKDF2 passphrase (local only). Includes bookmarks, notes, sessions, automation, tabs, history, clipboard, plugins, and settings.

@if (snap.settings.lastEncryptedBackupAt) {

Last export: {{ snap.settings.lastEncryptedBackupAt }}

}
Telemetry

When enabled, the main process may log a periodic diagnostic line. No data leaves your machine.

} @case ('advanced') {

Advanced

Developer options and data management.

@if (bridge.isElectron) {
Session

On close, saves a snapshot named "Auto-saved …" before exiting. May add a short delay.

}
Danger zone

These actions are irreversible. Make a backup before proceeding.

Clears tabs, bookmarks, history, and notes from this device.

} } } @else {

Loading settings…

}
@if (backupExportOpen()) {

Export encrypted backup

} @if (backupImportOpen()) {
@if (backupImportStep() === 'pass') {

Import encrypted backup

Enter the passphrase used when this file was exported.

} @else {

Apply backup

@if (persisted.snapshot()?.settings?.encryptedImportMode === 'merge_lww') { Merge imported data (last-write-wins for lists; local settings and open tabs are kept). } @else { Replace store sections wherever the backup contains data. }

}
} @if (resetDataConfirmOpen()) {

Reset local data?

This clears tabs, bookmarks, history, and notes on this device. The page will reload.

} @if (shortcutsResetConfirmOpen()) {

Reset all shortcuts?

All keyboard shortcuts will return to their defaults.

variables.rawdescription:
Renderer IPC channel strings inlined here on purpose: some dev bundles resolve
`@dev-lens/shared` in a way that drops newer keys from `IPC_CHANNELS`, which
produced `invoke(undefined)` at runtime. Keep in sync with `shared/src/ipc-channels.ts`
and `electron/preload.ts` ALLOWED_INVOKE.
variables.description: Renderer IPC channel strings inlined here on purpose: some dev bundles resolve @dev-lens/shared in a way that drops newer keys from IPC_CHANNELS, which produced invoke(undefined) at runtime. Keep in sync with shared/src/ipc-channels.ts and electron/preload.ts ALLOWED_INVOKE.

Renderer IPC channel strings inlined here on purpose: some dev bundles resolve
`@dev-lens/shared` in a way that drops newer keys from `IPC_CHANNELS`, which
produced `invoke(undefined)` at runtime. Keep in sync with `shared/src/ipc-channels.ts`
and `electron/preload.ts` ALLOWED_INVOKE.
